scope: Larger board-impact batch covering the next 24 unprojected players with strong returning production, high-major transfer relevance, or clear mid-major star roles.
method: Skip rows already present in earlier research batches.
Prioritize unprojected players with the strongest historical/player-impact anchors before moving into lower-confidence national roster depth.
Use official roster/bio/signing pages first, then reputable team coverage when official role context is thin.
Score projected starter/role from current-team roster context, returning hierarchy, production translation, and available staff comments.
Keep every row pending admin approval so the batch can be reviewed and applied in one pass.

formulas.projectedBbprWithHistory: historicalRating/proven production blended with projection score, then calibrated by player type

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Yes requires projected-lineup, start-history, returner hierarchy, or official/staff role language; Uncertain means strong minutes without a locked starting signal; No means depth/bench role.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: 90+ for clear starters/top options, 84-89 for likely starters or high-minute sixth men, 76-83 for rotation players with role caps.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Rewards teams with open usage/minutes or returning focal points; penalizes crowded high-major rotations.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Primary scorers/creators get 86+; floor spacers/bigs/connective pieces stay lower.
teamContextRubric.roleEvidence: Every row includes why starter credit was assigned or capped.

players.researchSummary: Johnson's Denver row had elite-usage inputs that overstated the national top-100 translation. The correction keeps the scorer profile but removes the top-100 role flag.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Johnson's Denver row had elite-usage inputs that overstated the national top-100 translation. The correction keeps the scorer profile but removes the top-100 role flag.
teamContext.roleCap: Corrected so role, anchor, and current trajectory are comparable across the full rated pool.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Primary scorer/creator after 35-plus MPG and league POY usage.
teamContext.scoredAnchor: Synthetic historical anchor: 66. Updated in the July 23 top-100 calibration pass.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Johnson with Denver.

players.researchSummary: Machowski was an efficient SoCon star, but UConn context points more to valuable shooting depth than a locked starter with Demary/Mullins/Ross/Hines around him. Score intentionally caps role credit despite strong Wofford production.

teamContext.starterEvidence: UConn roster and coverage frame him as an experienced backcourt addition, not a projected lead option.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely rotation shooter/secondary guard behind higher-usage UConn pieces.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Strong Wofford production, but high-major role uncertainty and crowded guard room cap upside.

evidence.notes: Official bio lists Second Team All-SoCon season with 17.2 PPG at Wofford.
Transfer context describes him as a key depth piece behind likely UConn guards.

players.researchSummary: Brown owns proven high-major guard production and should be a major Oklahoma backcourt piece, but OU roster competition with Nijel Pack/other guards keeps him below the clear-star tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Oklahoma roster context supports a prominent guard role.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starter or closing guard, but usage is shared on a transfer-heavy roster.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Strong A-10/OU production profile earns top-half starter credit.

evidence.notes: Career page lists All-A-10 honors and 15-point scoring profile.
Roster page places Brown on Oklahoma roster with veteran guards.

players.researchSummary: Simmons brings real scoring gravity to Villanova after ranking near the top of the A-10, but Villanova added multiple frontcourt/guard pieces and has Tyler Perkins/Devin Royal/Luigi Suigo context, so he gets impact-rotation credit rather than automatic star credit.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Villanova announcement emphasizes immediate shooting/scoring impact.
teamContext.roleCap: Crowded Villanova roster makes starting status plausible but not locked.
teamContext.positionCompetition: High shooting value; role capped because he is one of several new Wildcat scorers.

evidence.notes: Official signing notes 16.4 PPG at St. Bonaventure and three-level scoring.
Roster-addition article lists Simmons among a large newcomer group.

players.researchSummary: Speed projects as an FAU starter/lead guard after starting almost every game at Loyola Maryland and leading that roster in scoring and assists. The final score stays modest because the NBA/upside signal is not elite.

teamContext.starterEvidence: FAU profile gives strong starter evidence from 62 starts in 63 career games.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely primary/secondary creator on a newly shaped FAU roster.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Patriot League-to-AAC jump creates some translation risk.

evidence.notes: First-look article cites 15.3 PPG, 3.8 APG, and All-Patriot second team.
Roster lists Speed as junior guard transfer from Loyola Maryland.

players.researchSummary: Olsen was an elite Sun Belt shooter, but Auburn has Pettiford/Overton plus multiple portal additions, so his role is more floor-spacing rotation/spot starter than high-usage star.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Auburn official bio confirms major South Alabama production.
teamContext.roleCap: Projected role is capped by Auburn returning guards and transfer depth.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Shooting should travel, but usage likely falls at Auburn.

evidence.notes: Official bio lists Second Team All-Sun Belt, 15.7 PPG, and 102 made threes.
Auburn signing context notes backcourt led by Pettiford and Overton plus newcomers.

players.researchSummary: O Hara is a proven Horizon scorer moving within the league and should be a Wright State starter. Efficient rim pressure/physical guard value gets role credit, but lower NBA/upside scores keep him outside the national-board tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Wright State announcement calls him a proven league producer.

Roster lists CJ O Hara as junior guard transfer from Green Bay.

players.researchSummary: Cummings has real high-major scoring juice and was Pitt's leading scorer before injury, but the ankle issue and Memphis guard depth make starter credit uncertain. He gets upside/shot-creation value with a confidence cap.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Memphis announcement values his scoring punch.
teamContext.roleCap: Injury-shortened Pitt season reduces certainty entering Memphis.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Combo-guard scoring role likely, but not a locked top option.

evidence.notes: Official signing cites 432 Pitt points and major scoring flashes.
Pitt release says he led Panthers at 12.5 PPG before season-ending ankle procedure.

players.researchSummary: Gladden has one of the better young-transfer profiles in this batch after a 14.4/4.5 freshman season, but SDSU role certainty is slightly capped by Aztec guard depth and a tougher defensive/tempo environment.

players.researchSummary: Shelton is a high-volume shooter/scorer stepping up from Merrimack to Boston College. He can matter quickly, but ACC translation and BC roster uncertainty keep him below the safer starter tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Transfer report frames him as roster shooting boost.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely rotation wing with starting chance if shooting translates.

Merrimack roster confirms Shelton as a 6-5 guard from the prior roster.

players.researchSummary: Nadeau projects as a Tulane starter/major shooter after a Third Team All-Horizon season. Scoring efficiency is strong; national upside is limited by league translation and modest creation profile.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Tulane roster bio confirms Detroit Mercy production.

players.researchSummary: McBride is a skilled high-major forward addition after a James Madison breakout, but Kentucky has Moreno/NDiaye/Hawthorne/frontcourt depth, so he gets good rotation/possible starter credit rather than top-option credit.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Kentucky announcement confirms productive JMU starter profile.
teamContext.roleCap: Rotation forward with possible starting path depending on lineup.

Feature discusses fit with Moreno and role conversations in summer practice.

players.researchSummary: Wheeler-Thomas brings Summit MVP/tournament-lead-guard credentials to Boise State. He gets strong rotation credit, but Boise's backcourt depth and conference jump keep him outside top-100 range.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Boise State announcement confirms extensive starting history.
teamContext.roleCap: Starter or major guard is plausible but not locked.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Efficient veteran guard; not an elite NBA/upside profile.

evidence.notes: Official signing cites 92 career starts and 2026 Summit Tournament MVP.
Transfer coverage frames him as backcourt depth after 14.5 PPG season.

players.researchSummary: Enis deserves a stronger placement than a generic transfer: first-team American guard, elite volume shooting, and a clear Creighton need for veteran scoring. Still, Creighton adds BJ Davis/others, so he is not treated as a solo focal point.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Creighton offseason report lists Enis among key incoming veterans.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starter/closing shooter with heavy off-ball scoring role.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Scoring translates, but shared backcourt keeps burden below primary-star level.

evidence.notes: Creighton offseason report says Enis led USF with 16.4 PPG and was first-team all-league.
USF bio lists First Team American honors, 16.4 PPG, and 107 made threes.

players.researchSummary: Uelmen was a Summit breakout star and joins UNI with a strong chance to be a lead guard. He is below top-100 because of Missouri Valley/Summit translation, but his role and production should not be discounted.

teamContext.starterEvidence: UNI signing class highlights his scoring record and immediate impact.
